title: Fuel City
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Structure |image=[[File:Vlcsnap-2016-10-08-23h16m05s397.png]] |name=Fuel City |constructed= |destroyed= |type= |location=[[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]] |interest= |rebuilt= |builder= |height= |width= |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] }} {{Quote|Without a private ship, one would have to go to the nearest spaceport. That would be Fuel City. It's a tanker station and fuel depot, about 10 kilometers south of the city.|[[WeeGee]]|[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]]}} '''Fuel City''' was a fuel-tank complex that provided fuel to the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] near [[Barons Hed]] on [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]], ten [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] south of the spaceport. ==History== {{Quote|Look, flight control, I said I was sorry."
"Sorry doesn't count here in Fuel City, pilot.|[[Jan Ors]] and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Zyak]]|}} Fuel City must have been built after [[5 BBY/Legends|5 BBY]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' as [[Kyle Katarn]] didn't know its existence when he returned to his [[homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] [[5 ABY/Legends|after ten years]].''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' opening crawl The complex was guarded by Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] as well as hired [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]]. The vast workings of the complex was kept maintained by [[Ugnaught/Legends|Ugnaught]] workers. In that year, [[Gorc]] and [[Picaroon C. Boodle|Pic]], minions of the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]], were stationed in ''[[Sulon Star]]'', a [[freighter/Legends|cargo ship]] belonging to him; the ship was docked on station six, while a fully automated ship, and another much smaller ship occupied two other stations. After [[8t88/Legends|8t88]] completed his mission to Jerec, the Dark Jedi redirected him there to receive his "reward". [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[agent/Legends|agent]] Kyle Katarn was after him, and seeing his [[shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] leaving from Barons Hed, the ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]'' followed it. [[WeeGee]] guessed that it was heading to the station, being the quickiest way to leave the planet. Katarn came to the fuel station while attempting to [[Mission to Sulon|sneak aboard]] the ''Sulon Star''. Katarn landed on the roof and made his way into the station, past Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] as well as hired [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]]. The rebel made his way past the primary fuel station and shut down the fuel pipes in order to use them as a route to get to the refueling gantry where the ''Sulon Star'' was docked. Inside the ship, he discovered that the droid had been deactivated, and had to deal with the [[Brothers of the Sith]]; he defeated them and recovered the droid's head, but during that someone called for help. The ship, as well as the station's catwalks around it, were filled with Stormtroopers. [[Zyak]] and [[Nomo]] ordered to bleed the pipes and close the valves, but while the bleeding process was still only at seventy percent, and there was some relay problem with the valves, the ''Crow'' returned in order to extract Katarn. Zyak launched weapons from the [[Missile Battery|missile batteries]], but the ''Crow'' destroyed a [[storage tank sixteen|storage tank]] in order to distract the [[Heat-seeking missile/Legends|heat-seeking missile]]s in the explosion. More explosions followed as [[storage tank fifteen|three]] [[storage tank fourteen|nearby]] [[storage tank thirteen|tanks]] blew up. [[File:CrowExplosion.jpg|thumb|left|The ''Moldy Crow'' flees the exploding Fuel City.]] The ''Crow'' managed to extract Kyle from the top of the ''Sulon Star'' in the midst of exploging [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]] canisters around the station.''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIEs]] came after the ship, but the first one was shot down near a storage unit and crashed into a tank; one of its shrapnels punctured a line causing a leak, which then was set afire by flaming debris; the fiery liquid surrounded the maintainance facility. ==Layout== Fuel City was a massive fueling complex, consisting of nine fuel stations each with a refueling gantry. From distance, hundred of lights were seen randomly dotting the complex, and formings shadowy shapes. The stations had networks of interlocked [[tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]] that held the largest ships hovering in place while being served. Rows of storage tanks served all them, connected with a maze of huge fuel pipes to supply the large amounts needed for starships; some were so large that could be traversed by a [[humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] if emptied. There were at least forty-six valves. The primary fuel station was built as a large cylindrical building with a large fuel tank in the center. The fuel pipes connected to a refuelling gantry which where each guarded by several Imperial Stormtroopers.''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' Operations was responsible for the pumps, while switching was responsible for the valves. Safety regulations required that ships being served, had to maintain their locks, hatches and ports open, so that in case of a fire, [[autohose]]s could enter while the crew was escaping. Stops prevented the larger defensive weapons from firing on the fueling stations. ==Behind the scenes== In ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'', level 9: "Fuel Station Launch" is played inside the fuel station. The player heads through it to reach the Sulon Star. In the novel ''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'', Kyle Katarn doesn't venture through the fuel station to get to the ''Sulon Star'', but boards the ship via his own small ship, the [[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]. The fuel station was unnamed in the game, and received the name "Fuel City" in the novella as well as the refueling gantry which received the name "Station six".''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' In the game, some pipes near the end of the level, have three faces of [[Max (bunny)|Max]] as an [[Easter egg (virtual)|easter egg]]. ==Appearances== *''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' *[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]] *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Ciudad Combustible }} [[Category:Cities]] [[Category:Fueling locations]] [[Category:Galactic Empire locations]] [[Category:Sulon locations]]
